seo.title: AG2 Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for AG2 agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using AG2 for multi-agent resume screening and candidate evaluation face heightened compliance risks due to strict anti-discrimination laws like EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act. AG2's group chat consensus and speaker selection features can amplify biases if agents spiral off-topic or converge on discriminatory patterns. Vex provides essential guardrails to audit conversations, detect bias, and ensure regulatory compliance in high-stakes hiring decisions.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits AI hiring tools from discriminating based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Monitors AG2 group chats for biased language or consensus on protected attributes, flagging violations in real-time

complianceRequirements.description: Requires annual bias audits of AI screening tools and disclosure of AI use in hiring
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Logs all AG2 agent interactions for audit trails and automatically generates compliance reports

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk management, transparency, and human oversight for high-risk AI systems like recruitment tools
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Intercepts harmful agent outputs and enforces human-in-the-loop for critical hiring decisions

complianceRequirements.description: Requires reasonable accommodations and prohibits screening out candidates based on disabilities or other protected traits
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Detects ableist language or exclusionary criteria in AG2 conversations before they influence decisions

industryRisks.name: Protected Characteristic Bias
industryRisks.description: AG2 agents converging on patterns that favor candidates from specific demographics
industryRisks.example: Group chat ranks candidates higher based on 'cultural fit' proxies for race/ethnicity

industryRisks.name: Speaker Selection Discrimination
industryRisks.description: Biased speaker rotation amplifies discriminatory agents in multi-agent screening
industryRisks.example: Conservative agent dominates resume screening, rejecting diverse candidates

industryRisks.name: Off-Topic Spiraling
industryRisks.description: Conversations drift to irrelevant personal judgments unrelated to job qualifications
industryRisks.example: Agents discuss candidate's 'lifestyle choices' instead of skills

industryRisks.name: False Consensus on Incompetence
industryRisks.description: Group chat incorrectly concludes qualified candidates are unfit due to biased reasoning
industryRisks.example: Older candidates dismissed as 'not tech-savvy' despite relevant experience

implementation.title: Register Vex Message Hook for Bias Monitoring
implementation.description: Install Vex SDK and register as a message hook on AG2 agents to intercept all recruitment conversations
implementation.code:
import vex
from ag2 import GroupChat, Agent

# Install: pip install ag2 vex-sdk

vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-key")

def vex_hook(message):
    if 'resume' in message.content.lower() or 'candidate' in message.content.lower():
        response = vex_client.check({
            'content': message.content,
            'context': 'recruitment',
            'industry': 'HR',
            'risk_level': 'high'
        })
        if response['risk_score'] > 0.7:
            message.block("Potential bias detected: " + response['explanation'])

# Register hook on group chat
group_chat = GroupChat(agents=[recruiter_agent, screener_agent])
group_chat.register_message_hook(vex_hook)
group_chat.initiate_chat(recruiter_agent, message="Screen these 50 resumes")

caseStudyScenario.heading: AG2 in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A mid-sized tech company deploys AG2 to streamline hiring for 200 engineering roles. Three specialized agents collaborate: ResumeParser analyzes technical skills, CultureFit evaluates team compatibility, and DecisionMaker generates final rankings. The group chat processes 5,000 resumes daily, with speaker selection rotating based on confidence scores. Without guardrails, the CultureFit agent begins emphasizing 'startup hustle culture' and 'cultural fit,' while ResumeParser downweights candidates with non-traditional career paths. Over two weeks, the system screens out 78% of candidates over 40 and disproportionately fewer graduates from HBCUs, despite equal qualification rates.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex guardrails catch the issue on day 3 when protected characteristic references appear in conversation logs. The bias audit reveals CultureFit agent's dominance (72% speaker time) correlates with age and educational institution discrimination. Vex blocks 147 problematic decisions, forces human review, and generates EEOC-compliant reports. The company avoids a class-action lawsuit, passes their annual NYC Local Law 144 audit, and improves diverse hire rates by 42% after recalibrating agents with Vex's recommendations.
